title: cerasus vulgaris var. marasca
intro: Cerasus vulgaris var. marasca, also known as the Marasca cherry, is a variety of the wild cherry tree native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, dark red or almost black fruits. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. Consuming the fruit and leaves of the Marasca cherry has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive function. The herbal preparations of the Marasca cherry include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make medicinal teas, salves, and creams.
